mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, labor market tightness compresses wage growth slack and signals peak economic demand, driving equity risk premiums lower and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized household balance sheet expansion, which fuels aggregate demand and drives corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observed episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 103.0 signals that manufacturing capacity is fully utilized and approaching supply bottlenecks, which historically precedes margin compression and earnings revisions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 65% of cases when INDPRO crosses above 103.0, or the signal exhibits no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) across three consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates directly into higher corporate revenues, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 equity valuations over a 60-day forward window.
failure_condition: The sign of quarterly GDPC1 growth consistently fails to align with the sign of 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling correlation between GDPC1 changes and SPX forward returns drops below 0.10 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 2.5% annualized pace forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, raising real interest rates and compressing equity valuation multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following a CPI inflation spike above 2.5%, or the signal's information coefficient remains statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of episodes, or the cross-sectional predictive power of the interaction term vanishes (p > 0.15) in a rolling regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging past 1000 units while consumer credit expands indicates a broad-based credit-fueled housing boom that spills over into related industrial sectors and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of cases when both HOUST and RSAFS rise concurrently above their thresholds, or the joint signal's directional accuracy degrades to chance levels (50-52%) over a 4-year out-of-sample period.
